summary: Fixed CHANGES_REQUESTED finding from run 014-verification: moved FileManager synchronization from a caller-side global fmMu (catalog.go) into a narrow internal lock inside FileManager itself, and added a second narrow pageID-keyed striped lock in Catalog to preserve correctness for records that share a physical page.

new_lock_field.name: mu
new_lock_field.guards: highestAllocated (line ~66) and bitmap [PageSize]byte (line ~59), plus the brief bitmap-check/mutation critical sections inside AllocatePage (~line 121), FreePage (~line 156), and the highestAllocated read inside validDataPageID (~line 199). Does NOT guard the pread/pwrite/fsync syscalls in ReadPage/WritePage.
new_lock_field.helper_renamed: persistBitmap -> persistBitmapLocked (must be called with mu already held)

test_hook_field.name: writeDelay
test_hook_field.purpose: test-only hook invoked by WritePage right before its WriteAt+Sync I/O, after validDataPageID's brief mu section has released; nil in production, set only by file_test.go

fmMu_removed.evidence: grep -n fmMu engine/catalog/catalog.go returns no matches; readSlot/tombstone/insert/tryInsertInto call FileManager methods directly with no Catalog-side lock around them

new_lock_added_for_correctness.name: pageStripes
new_lock_added_for_correctness.location: engine/catalog/catalog.go (Catalog struct, ~line 130; const numPageStripes ~line 15; func pageStripeFor ~line 160)
new_lock_added_for_correctness.why: Not requested by the finding directly, but required: removing the global fmMu exposed a real, previously-masked torn-read/lost-update race in TestCatalogConcurrentDistinctFileIDs, because distinct fileIDs commonly share the same physical page and readSlot/tombstone/insert/tryInsertInto each do a non-atomic ReadPage->mutate->WritePage sequence. pageStripes (keyed by pageID % 256, orthogonal to the fileID-keyed stripes) makes each pageID's read-modify-write sequence atomic across different callers without reintroducing cross-fileID contention for fileIDs on different pages.

doc_comment_fixed.now_describes: stripes (fileID-keyed record-level lock), indexMu (index map RWMutex), pageStripes (pageID-keyed physical-page lock), and FileManager's own internal mu (bookkeeping-only, not exposed to Catalog)

contention_proof_test.name: TestCatalogFileManagerNarrowLockDoesNotSerializeAcrossIO
contention_proof_test.proves: AllocatePage and ReadPage (on a different, already-allocated page) both complete within a 2s bound while a separate WritePage call is deliberately held mid-I/O via the writeDelay hook, demonstrating FileManager's internal mu is not held for the duration of page I/O
